calendar.matters:
MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Plaintiff Yitong Wen appeared in pro per.
Steve Ngo, Esq. representing Defendant City of Daly City.

Plaintiff states that she only saw the tentative ruling of the Court this morning and did not provide notice to the other party. Plaintiff requests to contest the tentative ruling.
Defendant's Counsel states that the defense reserves the right to contest or object but is happy to proceed with a hearing.

Hearing held.

The Court hears argument from Plaintiff re: Code of Civil Procedure 394. Plaintiff alleges mistreatment from Defendant and believes that Plaintiff will not have a fair hearing if the case is transferred to San Mateo County.
Defendant's counsel responds to Plaintiff's argument focusing on this court's jurisdiction in terms of venue.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY OF DALY CITY MOTION TO TRANSFER

is GRANTED. Request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $2,400 in reasonable expenses and attorney's fees incurred in making the motion to transfer. Plaintiff to pay transfer fees.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiff'S Request For Contempt Of This Court And Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Plaintiff Yitong Wen appeared in pro per.
Steve Ngo, Esq. representing Defendant City of Daly City.

Plaintiff states that she only saw the tentative ruling of the Court this morning and did not provide notice to the other party. Plaintiff requests to contest the tentative ruling.
Defendant's Counsel states that the defense reserves the right to contest or object but is happy to proceed with a hearing.

Hearing held.

Arguments for the motion for transfer heard.

Plaintiff seeks clarification of the tentative ruling for contempt and sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF YITONG WEN Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiff'S Request For Contempt Of This Court And Sanctions

is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
